betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Mutant Beasts and Mobs§f

§aThe usual recipe for something harmless: gunpowder, spice, everything nice — then someone added §4Chemical X§f.§f
§4Mutant Creatures§f now roam the world, and they are §cvery much your problem§f. Mutants spawn rarely in the wild, each with unpleasant surprises and enhanced abilities.

§6Wild Spawn Rates§f:
Mutant variations spawn in vanilla biomes with the following relative frequencies:
§b• Mutant Zombie§f: Spawn Rate §e10§f (most common boss).
§b• Mutant Skeleton§f: Spawn Rate §e7§f.
§b• Mutant Creeper§f: Spawn Rate §e5§f.
§b• Mutant Enderman§f: Spawn Rate §e3§f (extremely rare and dangerous).

§6Boss Drops & Mechanics§f:
§b• Chemical X§f: A mysterious jar of mutagenic fluid. Throwing it at standard Zombies, Skeletons, Creepers, or Endermen has a chance to force them to mutate on the spot.
§b• Creeper Minion§f: Defeating a Mutant Creeper lets you hatch a loyal Creeper Minion. When fully grown, you can §aright-click§f to have it sit on your shoulder like a parrot.
§b• Special Loot§f: Defeating these beasts grants custom weapons like the §eHulk Hammer§f or §eEndersoul Hand§f.

§cWarning§f: Regular mobs can also spawn wearing §bNTM: CE armor§f and carrying §bNTM: CE weapons§f. §eThey look normal§f. §cThey are not§f. Some are equipped well enough to §4end bad decisions instantly§f.

§6The World Is Alive§f:
§aSmile.§f §cAnd it wants you dead.§f
